title: Security Metrics
cert_objective: CySA+ CS0-003 Domain 4 — Reporting and Communication
scenario:
AEGIS TERMINAL v1.0 -- VERIDIAN SYSTEMS SOC

INCOMING TICKET -- SECURITY METRICS TEAM
PRIORITY: MEDIUM
ANALYST: YOU

OPERATION IRONCLAD -- SECURITY METRICS BASELINE

As part of Operation IRONCLAD, you have been asked to calculate Veridian's
security metrics baseline for the past quarter. Three incidents have been
selected as the metric sample set.

INCIDENT DATA:

  INC-001:
    Compromised:  2026-03-01 00:00  (NIGHTWIRE initial access)
    Detected:     2026-03-22 12:00  (21.5 days later -- slow detection)
    Resolved:     2026-03-24 12:00

  INC-002:
    Compromised:  2026-03-15 10:00  (phishing-based credential theft)
    Detected:     2026-03-15 12:00  (2 hours -- fast detection)
    Resolved:     2026-03-16 12:00

  INC-003:
    Compromised:  2026-03-28 00:00  (unpatched web server exploit)
    Detected:     2026-03-30 00:00  (48 hours -- medium detection time)
    Resolved:     2026-04-01 12:00

Type 'tools' to calculate MTTD and MTTR across all three incidents.

What is the Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) across all three incidents,
rounded to the nearest hour?
challenge: What is the Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) across all three incidents, rounded to the nearest hour?
hints:
Go to https://www.crowdstrike.com/cybersecurity-101/mean-time-to-detect-mttd/
        Read about MTTD (Mean Time to Detect) and MTTR (Mean Time to Respond).
        These are the two most important security metrics for demonstrating
        detection and response effectiveness to management.

MTTD = average time from initial compromise to detection.
        For each incident: MTTD_i = hours from 'Compromised' to 'Detected'
        Then average all MTTD values.
        INC-001: March 1 00:00 to March 22 12:00 = 21 days + 12h = 516h
        INC-002: March 15 10:00 to March 15 12:00 = 2h
        INC-003: March 28 00:00 to March 30 00:00 = 48h
        Type 'tools' to compute the average.

Type 'tools' in the game. The metrics calculator computes per-incident
        MTTD and MTTR, then averages them. The average MTTD is your answer.
        Average = sum of all MTTD values / number of incidents.

SPOILER -- MTTD = (516 + 2 + 48) / 3 = 566 / 3 = 188.67 -> 189 hours.
        INC-001 heavily skews the average upward -- the NIGHTWIRE incident
        went undetected for 21.5 days (516h). This is why a single slow
        detection event can make an organization look much worse on metrics.
        Type: 189
tools: Computes per-incident MTTD (compromise to detection) and MTTR (detection to resolution) from timestamp data, then calculates aggregate averages for the metric report.
learn:
Security metrics translate technical security activities into business language.
MTTD and MTTR are the two most commonly reported security KPIs.

Mean Time to Detect (MTTD):
  Definition: average time from initial compromise to security team detection.
  Formula: sum of (detected_at - compromised_at) for all incidents / incident count
  Units: hours
  Better = lower. Target: industry leaders achieve < 24h MTTD.

Mean Time to Respond (MTTR):
  Definition: average time from detection to containment/resolution.
  Formula: sum of (resolved_at - detected_at) for all incidents / incident count
  Units: hours
  Better = lower.

Industry benchmarks (CrowdStrike 2023 Global Threat Report):
  Average MTTD for all incidents: ~21 days
  Top quartile MTTD: < 24 hours
  Average MTTR: ~5 days
  Best-in-class MTTR: < 1 hour

Other security metrics:
  Patch compliance rate:    % of systems patched within SLA window
  SLA adherence rate:       % of incidents resolved within SLA
  Vulnerability closure rate: % of critical vulns remediated within 30 days

Metric improvement strategies:
  Lower MTTD: better threat detection rules, EDR coverage, UEBA
  Lower MTTR: playbook automation, SOAR platform, pre-approved response actions

debrief.summary: MTTD = 189 hours (~7.9 days). The average is heavily skewed by INC-001 (NIGHTWIRE), which went undetected for 516 hours (21.5 days). INC-002 was detected in just 2 hours -- excellent detection. MTTR = 44 hours -- faster than the industry average of ~5 days, indicating the response team is effective once detection occurs.
debrief.real_world: MTTD and MTTR are reported quarterly to CISOs and annually to boards in most enterprise security programs. IBM Cost of a Data Breach Report 2023: identifying a breach in < 200 days saves an average of $1.02M compared to > 200 days.
debrief.next_step:
Read the IBM Cost of a Data Breach Report 2023:
https://www.ibm.com/reports/data-breach
debrief.cert_link:
CySA+ CS0-003 Domain 4 -- Reporting and Communication:
"Given a scenario, calculate and interpret security metrics including MTTD, MTTR, and patch compliance rate."
debrief.exam_tip: On the exam, MTTD = time from initial compromise (or initial access) to detection. MTTR = time from detection to resolution/containment. Both are averages across multiple incidents. Lower = better. Know that MTTD measures detection effectiveness and MTTR measures response effectiveness. A high MTTD indicates detection capability problems; a high MTTR indicates response process problems.
